text: The air is thick with stillness, punctuated by the hum of fluorescent lights and distant reflections on glass. The tilted angles and shadows give the place an unsettling, liminal feeling. I am alone in this corridor, the silence is almost tangible. I feel like I am on the threshold of some transition, caught between an ending and an unknown beginning. There is a muted anticipation, yet a faint sense of unease lingers; perhaps I’m not supposed to be here, or simply that this moment of waiting magnifies my awareness.
